meta.name: Forgotten Industries
meta.tagline: An archive and lab journal exploring what happens to the things we leave behind: old machines, abandoned projects, and the parts of ourselves we once thought lost.
meta.voice: intimate, precise, archival, technical, emotionally honest

projects.title: CaseLabs Mercury S8 + Pedestal Restoration
projects.working_title: Rebuilding and Archiving a CaseLabs Mercury S8 + Pedestal 2014 Time Capsule
projects.summary: Documentation and restoration of a white powder-coated CaseLabs Mercury S8 with pedestal, stored for roughly a decade and returned to the bench as part hardware archive, part recovery log, part proof of life.

origin.context: The project paused after Matthew's father died and returned in 2026 as part of rebuilding life, hobbies, and identity.

layout_goals: Clean serial loop before experimenting with complexity

route: Down through midplate to pedestal via QDCs
preferences: Barbs and clamps over excessive angled fittings
Hardline or copper possible later as aesthetic evolution

documentation_style: Mark unknown parts temporarily with removable labels
Write logs as observations, not just progress updates

projects.title: Garage Archaeology
projects.summary: Recovered objects, parts, machines, tools, and forgotten projects treated as artifacts. The record begins before the object is cleaned or assigned a final meaning.
documentation_style: Record storage state, condition, missing hardware, and first hypotheses

projects.title: Lab Journal
projects.summary: Process notes, experiments, methods, failures, findings, and design decisions for technical recovery through craft.
documentation_style: Use methods, observations, and findings when they help
Keep reflection grounded in objects and actions
Let the technical documentation carry the emotion

inventory.name: CaseLabs Mercury S8
inventory.notes: White powder-coated Mercury S8. First pass should identify panels, fasteners, window configuration, and any missing hardware before cleaning.

inventory.name: CaseLabs pedestal
inventory.notes: Pedestal planned for radiator capacity, QDC pass-throughs, and service drain. Confirm exact accessory configuration before layout decisions.

inventory.name: EK XTX 360 radiator
inventory.condition: used / needs cleaning and leak test
inventory.notes: Heavy cooling overhead from the original watercooling plan. Needs flush, exterior cleaning, thread inspection, and leak test.

inventory.name: EK 360 slim radiator
inventory.condition: used / needs cleaning and leak test
inventory.notes: Thin 360 radiator present in the recovered cooling inventory. Confirm exact model and fitment.

inventory.name: Noiseblocker eLoop white PWM fans
inventory.notes: Test bearing noise, PWM response, cable condition, and visual yellowing. Decide later which units remain with the archive.

inventory.name: Aqua Computer D5 Aquabus pumps
inventory.notes: Intended for a dual-pump layout with Aquaero control. Test before any loop planning becomes final.

inventory.name: DDC pumps with EK clear tops
inventory.notes: Identify exact pump variants and top condition. Record unknowns rather than guessing.

inventory.name: Aquaero 6 XT
inventory.notes: Controller for pumps, fans, and sensors. Confirm display, buttons, headers, and Aquabus behavior.

inventory.name: Large EK cylinder reservoir
inventory.notes: Intended to remain visible through the right-side window. Inspect acrylic, threads, brackets, and O-rings.

fieldLogs.title: Rebuilding and Archiving a CaseLabs Mercury S8 + Pedestal 2014 Time Capsule

sections.heading: Condition Found
sections.body: White powder-coated chassis and pedestal recovered after roughly a decade in storage. The object should be photographed as found before dust, missing fasteners, or provisional assemblies are disturbed.

sections.heading: Observations
sections.body: The case appears to be the core archive object. Some STH-10 accessories may be present. Watercooling parts include EK 360 radiators, Aqua Computer D5 Aquabus pumps, DDC pumps with clear tops, an Aquaero 6 XT, a large EK cylinder reservoir, and approximately twenty or more white Noiseblocker eLoop PWM fans.

sections.heading: Methods
sections.body: Identify panel by panel. Assign temporary removable labels to unknown parts. Separate fasteners by location. Photograph each assembly before cleaning. Record uncertainty directly instead of smoothing it out.

sections.heading: Restoration Notes
sections.body: Start with a serviceable serial loop using EPDM tubing, barbs, and clamps. Keep the pedestal radiator path and quick-disconnect pass-throughs accessible. Function comes first; copper or hardline can become an aesthetic evolution later.

sections.heading: What It Means / Reflection
sections.body: This is not only a computer case. It is an interrupted map. The restoration matters because it turns private discontinuity into visible evidence: the hands still know how to build.

sections.heading: Next Actions
sections.body: Complete photo pass. Confirm exact panel configuration. Leak-test radiators. Test fans and pumps. Build the inventory record before deciding what stays, what sells, and what remains archive only.

sections.heading: Principle
sections.body: Recovery starts with the object as found. Dust, missing hardware, bad assumptions, and provisional assemblies are part of the record.

sections.heading: Method
sections.body: Photograph the object before cleaning. Label uncertainty. Record what is visible, what is missing, and what cannot yet be known.

sections.heading: Finding
sections.body: The technical record protects the emotional truth from performance. Evidence first. Interpretation later.
